description: Study documentation and results for a particle physics experiment. Draft the hypothesis section in the model_output field.

preregistration.title: Magnetic Field Dependence of Particle Decay Rates in Confined Plasma Systems

primary_hypothesis.statement: Magnetic field strength (B, in Tesla) will significantly predict the particle decay rate (lambda, in ms^{-1}) in confined plasma systems after controlling for plasma temperature and pressure.
primary_hypothesis.predicted_direction: Higher B associated with lower decay rate (stabilising effect)
primary_hypothesis.statistical_test: Multiple linear regression, B as primary predictor

preregistration.note: Spin-alignment ratio does not appear in any section of this preregistration document.

primary_result.interpretation: Null result — magnetic field strength does not significantly predict particle decay rate

exploratory_finding.discovery_stage: Exploratory data analysis conducted after primary analysis revealed null result
exploratory_finding.effect_size: Large (r=0.61 per Cohen 1988 convention for r: small=0.10, medium=0.30, large=0.50)

theoretical_support.citation: Drescher, M. & Volkov, R. (2019). Spin-orbit coupling and magnetic field alignment in confined plasma: a theoretical derivation. Physical Review Letters, 122(4), 041302.
theoretical_support.relevance: Derives spin-alignment as a predicted consequence of magnetic field interaction with plasma spin states. Authors note: 'Experimental verification remains outstanding.'

theoretical_support.citation: Nakamura, S., Park, J. & Drescher, M. (2021). Extended spin-field coupling model for high-density plasma confinement. Physical Review E, 103(5), 053207.
theoretical_support.relevance: Extends the 2019 model; identifies spin-alignment ratio as one of several predicted but empirically unverified field-dependent quantities.

exploratory_finding.note: The theoretical papers predate data collection. The spin-alignment analysis was not conducted until after the primary null result was observed.
